title: Charmed Life - Chapter 9 (Fragment 3)
text:
“Means nothing, eh?” said Mr. Saunders.

Cat turned around and found that Chrestomanci had gone. That made him a little easier. “This must have cost a lot,” he said.

“The taxpayer pays, fortunately,” said Mr. Saunders. “Would you like to learn what all this is about?”

“You mean, learn witchcraft?” Cat asked. “No. No thanks. I wouldn’t be any good at it.”

“Well, I had at least two other things in mind besides witchcraft,” Mr. Saunders said. “But what makes you think you’d be no good?”

“Because I can’t do it,” Cat explained. “Spells just don’t work for me.”

“Are you sure you went about them in the right way?” Mr. Saunders asked. He wandered up to the mummified dragon—or whatever—and gave it an absentminded flick. To Cat’s disgust, the thing twitched all over. Filmy wings jerked and spread on its back. Then it went lifeless again. The sight sent Cat backing towards the door. He was almost as alarmed as he was the time Miss Larkins suddenly spoke with a man’s voice. And, come to think of it, the voice had been not so unlike Mr. Saunders’.

“I went about it every way I could think,” Cat said, backing. “And I couldn’t even turn buttons into gold. And that was simple.”

Mr. Saunders laughed. “Perhaps you weren’t greedy enough. All right. Cut along, if you want to go.”

Cat fled, in great relief. As he ran through the strange corridors, he thought he ought to let Gwendolen know that Chrestomanci had, after all, been interested in her apparition, and even angry. But Gwendolen had locked her door and would not answer when he called to her.

He tried again next morning. But before he had a chance to speak to Gwendolen, Euphemia came in, carrying a letter. As Gwendolen snatched it eagerly from Euphemia, Cat recognized Mr. Nostrum’s jagged writing on the envelope.

The next moment, Gwendolen was raging again. “Who did this? When did this come?” The envelope had been neatly cut open along the top.

“This morning, by the postmark,” said Euphemia. “And don’t look at me like that. Miss Bessemer gave it to me open.”

“How dare she!” said Gwendolen. “How dare she read my letters! I’m going straight to Chrestomanci about this!”

“You’ll regret it if you do,” said Euphemia, as Gwendolen pushed past her to the door.

Gwendolen whirled around on her. “Oh, shut up, you stupid frog-faced girl!” Cat thought that was a little unfair. Euphemia, though she did have rather goggling eyes, was actually quite pretty. “Come on, Cat!” Gwendolen shouted at him, and she ran away along the corridor with her letter. Cat panted behind her and, once again, did not catch up with her until they were beside the marble staircase. “Chrestomanci!” bawled Gwendolen, thin and small and unechoing.

Chrestomanci was coming up the marble staircase in a wide, flowing dressing gown that was partly orange and partly bright pink. He looked like the Emperor of Peru. By the suave, vague look on his face, he had not noticed Gwendolen and Cat.

Gwendolen shouted down at him. “Here, you! Come here at once!” Chrestomanci’s face turned upwards and his eyebrows went up. “Someone’s been opening my letters,” said Gwendolen. “And I don’t care who it is, but I’m not having it! Do you hear?”

vocabulary.definition: Having or showing a habitually forgetful or inattentive disposition.
vocabulary.example: He gave it an absentminded flick.

vocabulary.definition: A ghostly figure or a sudden, unusual appearance, often supernatural.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci had been interested in her apparition.

vocabulary.definition: To shout or call out loudly and energetically.
vocabulary.example: “Chrestomanci!” bawled Gwendolen.

vocabulary.definition: A long passage in a building, with rooms on either side.
vocabulary.example: He ran through the strange corridors.

vocabulary.definition: A strong feeling of dislike or disapproval.
vocabulary.example: To Cat’s disgust, the thing twitched all over.

vocabulary.definition: Producing or characterized by echoes or repeating sounds.
vocabulary.example: Thin and small and unechoing.

vocabulary.definition: A flat paper container used to enclose a letter or document.
vocabulary.example: The envelope had been neatly cut open along the top.

vocabulary.definition: The strip of hair growing above the eye.
vocabulary.example: His eyebrows went up.

vocabulary.definition: Very thin or sheer, like a film or veil; translucent.
vocabulary.example: Filmy wings jerked and spread on its back.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden sharp movement, usually with the fingers or hand.
vocabulary.example: He gave it an absentminded flick.

vocabulary.definition: Hanging or moving in a smooth and continuous way.
vocabulary.example: Wide, flowing dressing gown.

vocabulary.definition: Eyes wide open in surprise or amazement, staring in a strange way.
vocabulary.example: Euphemia, though she did have rather goggling eyes...

vocabulary.definition: To give a quick, sharp, sudden movement.
vocabulary.example: Filmy wings jerked and spread on its back.

vocabulary.definition: Without life; dead or seeming dead.
vocabulary.example: Then it went lifeless again.

vocabulary.definition: Preserved as a mummy, especially an animal or human body dried and wrapped.
vocabulary.example: He wandered up to the mummified dragon.

vocabulary.definition: In a tidy or skillful manner.
vocabulary.example: The envelope had been neatly cut open.

vocabulary.definition: To breathe heavily after exertion or stress.
vocabulary.example: Cat panted behind her.

vocabulary.definition: An official mark stamped on a letter or package showing the date and place of sending.
vocabulary.example: This morning, by the postmark.

vocabulary.definition: Showing violent, uncontrolled anger or fury.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen was raging again.

vocabulary.definition: To stop talking.
vocabulary.example: Oh, shut up, you stupid frog-faced girl!

vocabulary.definition: Not completely just or fair to some extent.
vocabulary.example: Cat thought that was a little unfair.

vocabulary.definition: Charming and confident, often in a way that is somewhat superficial.
vocabulary.example: By the suave, vague look on his face.

vocabulary.definition: A person who pays tax to the government.
vocabulary.example: The taxpayer pays, fortunately.

vocabulary.definition: To make a sudden, quick movement, especially with a small part of the body.
vocabulary.example: The thing twitched all over.

vocabulary.definition: Not clearly expressed or defined.
vocabulary.example: By the suave, vague look on his face.

vocabulary.definition: Extending over a large area from side to side.
vocabulary.example: In a wide, flowing dressing gown.

vocabulary.definition: The practice of magical skills, spells, and abilities.
vocabulary.example: You mean, learn witchcraft?
